classes.description: Assembler path, linker path, and arch-specific as/ld flag lists. Plain Python class (not pydantic) — constructed directly by toolchain_for.

functions.name: build_guest
functions.docstring: Assemble and link the guest image for the given arch/platform. Returns path to the output binary.

functions.name: toolchain_for
functions.docstring: Return the Toolchain (assembler, linker, as_flags, ld_flags) for the (arch, platform) target. Raises ValueError on unsupported combinations.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test toolchain_for with all supported (arch, platform) combos plus rejection cases. Functional test build_guest with a minimal .S file that assembles and links successfully. Test missing-toolchain path by mocking subprocess.run to raise FileNotFoundError.

modules.name: test_runner
modules.responsibility: Orchestrate the full test cycle: build guest, launch VM, wait for ready, run test cases, kill VM, report results. Serial output read in binary mode with errors='replace'. This is the entry point for both pre-commit and CI.

classes.name: TestCase
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-case` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestResult
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-result` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestSuite
classes.description: Collection of test cases for a target

classes.name: SuiteResult
classes.description: Aggregate results for a test suite run. Has all_passed property.

functions.name: run_suite
functions.docstring: Run a complete test suite: build, boot, test, kill, report. Skips gracefully if Firecracker without KVM.

functions.name: check_serial
functions.docstring: Check serial output for expected content. Reads in binary mode with errors='replace'.

functions.name: check_http
functions.docstring: Make HTTP GET request and verify response body matches expected.

functions.name: _build_image
functions.docstring: Build guest image. Return Path on success or TestResult on failure.

functions.name: _boot_and_test
functions.docstring: Wait for ready, run cases, return results.

functions.name: _should_skip
functions.docstring: Return skip reason if suite can't run, or None if runnable.

functions.name: _run_case
functions.docstring: Run a single test case against a booted VM.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test check_serial and check_http with fixtures. Integration test run_suite with mocked build/launch/wait/kill. Test all failure paths: build failure, boot timeout, check mismatch, Firecracker skip without KVM.

modules.name: cli
modules.responsibility: Command-line interface for the test harness. Supports running a single arch/platform combo or the full matrix. Reports pass/fail per test with exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any failure.

functions.name: main
functions.docstring: Entry point. Parse args, run tests, report, return exit code.

functions.name: parse_args
functions.docstring: Parse command-line arguments.

functions.name: _load_suite
functions.docstring: Load a test suite from a JSON file.

functions.name: _print_result
functions.docstring: Print test results for one suite.

modules.test_strategy: Test argument parsing. Test exit codes for all-pass and any-failure scenarios with mocked test_runner. Test suite loading from JSON.

external_dependencies.name: pydantic
external_dependencies.reason: Data models for VMConfig, VMHandle, TestSuite, TestCase, TestResult, SuiteResult. field_validator for path/args validation. Literal type for platform.

verification_cases.name: eth-fcs-primitive
verification_cases.rationale: Unit-level verification of the CRC-32 IEEE 802.3 primitive on both arches against zlib vectors. Virtio-net frame-level FCS behaviour (eth-fcs-virtio) is separate.

verification_cases.name: virtio-init-sequence
verification_cases.rationale: Integration verification of the full Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 device init handshake. Current tracer-bullet marker checks in run_local.sh are the de-facto cover, but a dedicated integration test that asserts on both happy-path markers and each .*_fail endpoint hasn't been written yet.

entities.name: GuestImage
entities.description: An assembled and linked bare-metal binary that boots directly in a VM. Produced by GNU as + GNU ld from arch/<isa>/platform/<vm>/ sources.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Filesystem path to the ELF/flat binary. Validated: no '..' traversal, resolved to absolute.

properties.name: arch
properties.description: Target ISA

properties.name: platform
properties.description: Target VM platform. Literal type -- Pydantic rejects invalid values.

entities.name: VMInstance
entities.description: A running QEMU or Firecracker process hosting a guest image. Launched in the background, polled for readiness, killed cleanly after tests complete. Popen object tracked in a thread-safe registry keyed by PID. Never use timeout to kill QEMU -- background + poll + kill.

properties.name: pid
properties.description: Host OS process ID. Popen object stored separately in _proc_registry (not Pydantic-serializable).

properties.name: serial_path
properties.description: Path to serial output file (-serial file:<path>). Validated: no '..' traversal. Truncated on launch.

properties.name: stderr_path
properties.description: Path to VMM stderr capture file. Created alongside serial_path as <serial_path>.stderr. Truncated on launch.

entities.name: TestCase
entities.description: A single verification to run against a booted VM guest. Checks are performed from the host side -- either parsing serial output or making HTTP requests to the guest.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human-readable test name

properties.name: check_type
properties.description: serial: check serial output file for expected markers. http: make HTTP request and verify response.

properties.name: expected
properties.description: Expected output or response content

entities.name: TestResult
entities.description: Pass/fail outcome of a single test case.

properties.name: actual
properties.description: Actual output received

properties.name: message
properties.description: Failure detail if not passed

entities.name: FSATransition
entities.description: A single state transition in an FSA automaton under D043's runtime model. One transition is the unit against which FSA_TRANSITION_BUDGET_NS is enforced: handler-body execution plus dispatch bookkeeping.

properties.name: from_state
properties.description: Current state index before the transition.

properties.name: event_code
properties.description: Event that triggered the transition.

properties.name: to_state
properties.description: State index the automaton moves to.

entities.name: EthernetFrame
entities.description: A single Ethernet II frame crossing the L2 boundary. Per ETH-001..ETH-005, fixed 14-byte header + 46..1500 payload + 4-byte FCS. The unit against which the L2 throughput budgets (1 Gbps floor, 10 Gbps target per D040) are enforced on both RX and TX paths.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Total frame length including header and FCS, in bytes. 64..1518 untagged, 64..1522 with VLAN.

properties.name: ether_type
properties.description: Big-endian 16-bit EtherType (≥ 0x0600) or 802.3 length (< 0x0600; out of scope per ETH-002).

entities.name: VirtioNetDevice
entities.description: The virtio-net device exposed by Firecracker at MMIO 0xC0001000 (MEM_32BIT_DEVICES per the VMM layout). The L2 driver owns the full Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 init sequence (reset → ACKNOWLEDGE → DRIVER → feature negotiation → FEATURES_OK → virtqueue init → DRIVER_OK) plus the split-virtqueue descriptor / available / used rings that upper layers consume for RX and TX.

properties.name: mmio_base
properties.description: Physical MMIO base address. 0xC0001000 on Firecracker x86_64 per MEM_32BIT_DEVICES.

properties.name: queue_size
properties.description: Driver-negotiated virtqueue size, min(QueueNumMax, VIRTQ_MAX_SIZE=256). Both RX and TX queues share this value in MVP.

entities.name: ARPPacket
entities.description: A single ARP packet per RFC 826, carried inside an Ethernet frame with EtherType 0x0806. Fixed-layout header (HTYPE, PTYPE, HLEN, PLEN, OP) followed by Sender HW / Sender Protocol / Target HW / Target Protocol addresses.

properties.name: operation
properties.description: OP field: 1=REQUEST, 2=REPLY (RFC 826).

properties.name: sender_hw_addr
properties.description: 6-byte Sender Hardware Address (MAC).

properties.name: sender_protocol_addr
properties.description: 4-byte Sender Protocol Address (IPv4).

properties.name: target_hw_addr
properties.description: 6-byte Target Hardware Address (MAC). Zero-filled on REQUEST, populated on REPLY.

properties.name: target_protocol_addr
properties.description: 4-byte Target Protocol Address (IPv4). Anchor for ARP-004 / ARP-011 local-IP checks.

entities.name: ObservabilityEventSite
entities.description: A single OBS_EMIT macro instance in the runtime code. Each site bears a fixed cost when observability is disabled, a slightly higher cost when enabled but category-gated-off, and the intrinsic emission cost when both verbosity and category pass. Per the observability proposal (docs/observability.md, pre-D050) and D046's assembly-deferral bar, the disabled-path cost must stay inside a tight budget so shipping observability everywhere doesn't erode the L2 frame-rate target.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category name: core / uart / virtio / fsa / eth / arp / ip / tcp / http / timer / perf_sample / reserved.

properties.name: min_level
properties.description: Lowest verbosity level at which this site emits (0=off, 1=error, 2=warn, 3=info, 4=debug, 5=trace).

relationships.name: boots
relationships.description: A VM instance boots exactly one guest image

relationships.name: runs_against
relationships.description: Test cases run against a booted VM instance

relationships.name: produces
relationships.description: Each test case produces one result

domain_constraints.name: no-native-execution
domain_constraints.description: Assembly code must NEVER be executed natively on the host. All execution happens inside a VM (QEMU or Firecracker).
domain_constraints.rationale: D003 (100% assembly) — host-native execution of bare-metal targets is undefined and dangerous; a VM is the boundary.

domain_constraints.name: clean-vm-kill
domain_constraints.description: Never use timeout to kill QEMU. Launch in background, poll serial output file, then kill cleanly via Popen.terminate/kill when registry entry exists, or bare PID signals with waitpid as fallback. Use -serial file:<path> not -serial mon:stdio.
domain_constraints.rationale: CLAUDE.md QEMU gotchas: `timeout` doesn't flush `-serial file:` output; mon:stdio breaks under stdout redirection.

domain_constraints.name: host-side-verification
domain_constraints.description: All test verification is from the host side. The guest has no test infrastructure inside it -- it just does what it does, and the host observes. Serial output read in binary mode with errors='replace' to handle non-UTF-8.
domain_constraints.rationale: Bare-metal assembly targets can't host pytest; the host observes serial output + exit signals and interprets them as test outcomes.

domain_constraints.name: kvm-required-for-firecracker
domain_constraints.description: Firecracker requires /dev/kvm and only works for the native architecture. Skip gracefully if KVM is not available.
domain_constraints.rationale: GHA hosted arm64 runners don't expose /dev/kvm, so the aarch64/firecracker cell can't run in CI; it's exercised locally via the Pi-side tracer bullet instead.

domain_constraints.name: path-traversal-rejected
domain_constraints.description: All file paths (image_path, serial_path) are validated to reject '..' components and resolved to absolute form. Pydantic field_validator enforces at construction.
domain_constraints.rationale: Defensive against accidental or adversarial path traversal in test-harness inputs; pydantic validation is the single chokepoint all VM configs pass through.

domain_constraints.name: blocked-qemu-args
domain_constraints.description: extra_args on VMConfig are validated against a blocklist of dangerous QEMU flags: -monitor, -vnc, -chardev, -netdev, -nic, -spice. Pydantic field_validator rejects at construction.
domain_constraints.rationale: Blocklist prevents test harnesses from opening monitor/vnc/network surfaces that could expose the host. Noted as brittle (allowlist would be stronger) in Gemini review; developer-tool scope keeps blocklist acceptable for now.

domain_constraints.name: thread-safe-registry
domain_constraints.description: The Popen process registry is protected by threading.Lock. The Python harness runs on Linux where concurrent launch_vm/kill_vm from different threads is a real scenario.
domain_constraints.rationale: Python harness is host-side code running on Linux under stdlib threading; concurrent launch_vm/kill_vm from different threads is a real scenario (pytest-xdist, concurrent integration runs). Bare-metal single-threaded FSA reasoning does NOT apply here per `feedback_tooling_is_not_target.md`.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-001
domain_constraints.description: On init, reset the device by writing 0 to the Device Status MMIO register (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 1). Post-reset state verified via the shared .status_fail endpoint (VIO-003).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-002
domain_constraints.description: Set ACKNOWLEDGE (bit 0) in Status after reset (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 2). Post-ACK state verified via the shared .status_fail endpoint (VIO-003).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-003
domain_constraints.description: Set DRIVER (bit 1) in Status after ACKNOWLEDGE (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 3). Full expected state after steps 1-3 cross-checked via equality compare against ACK|DRIVER (0x03); mismatch routes through .status_fail and VIO-009.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-004
domain_constraints.description: Read device feature bits via DeviceFeaturesSel / DeviceFeatures, select the driver's subset, write it back via DriverFeaturesSel / DriverFeatures (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 4). MVP subset is VIRTIO_F_VERSION_1 only; VIO-F-001 governs it, VIO-F-002 and VIO-F-006 are declared deviations, and VIO-F-003..005/007 remain in REQUIREMENTS.md pending formalisation into the ontology.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-005
domain_constraints.description: Set FEATURES_OK (bit 3) in Status after driver-features write (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 5).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-006
domain_constraints.description: Re-read Status; MUST abort init if FEATURES_OK is not still set (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 6). Equality compare against ACK|DRIVER|FEATURES_OK catches both FEATURES_OK-cleared and unexpected stray bits.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-007
domain_constraints.description: Read device-specific config, discover + initialize virtqueues (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 7). Slice 1: QueueSel + QueueNumMax discovery emits QUEUES:RX=<hex> TX=<hex>. Slice 2: QueueNum (clamped to VIRTQ_MAX_SIZE=256), QueueDesc / QueueDriver / QueueDevice physical addresses programmed, QueueReady toggled with read-back verify. Emits QUEUES:READY on success.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 7 + §4.2.2 register semantics. Memory allocation per D043 (static pools).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-008
domain_constraints.description: Set DRIVER_OK (bit 2) in Status; device is now live (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 8). Read-back equality-compares against ACK|DRIVER|FEATURES_OK|DRIVER_OK (0x0F); mismatch routes through .driver_ok_fail + VIO-009.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-009
domain_constraints.description: On any fatal driver error, set FAILED (bit 7) in Status (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2). Shared .set_failed helper is the single halt endpoint for every post-MagicValue failure path — .status_fail, .features_fail_no_v1, .features_fail_rejected, .queue_fail_rx, .queue_fail_tx, .queue_ready_fail, .driver_ok_fail all route through it. .virtio_fail bypasses it: on a MagicValue mismatch the device's register layout is unverified and a Status write would be blind.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-001
domain_constraints.description: MUST negotiate VIRTIO_F_VERSION_1 (bit 32) — we are a modern driver (Virtio 1.2 §6). MVP driver-features subset is VERSION_1 only; everything else (MAC / STATUS / MQ / CSUM / GSO / EVENT_IDX) stays off until the code path that consumes it lands.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-002
domain_constraints.description: MUST negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_MAC (bit 5) if the device offers it, and read MAC from config space (Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3).
domain_constraints.rationale: DEVIATION: MVP feature-negotiation commit (2026-04-19) accepts no device-specific features. MAC read is deferred to the VIO-007 device-config follow-up. Until that lands, the driver acts as if the device did not offer MAC — a locally-administered random MAC would be generated if this shipped. Tracked so it doesn't silently become a latent bug.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-006
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD NOT negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_CSUM / VIRTIO_NET_F_GUEST_CSUM unless our TCP/UDP stack handles partial checksums (Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3).
domain_constraints.rationale: DEVIATION by spec intent: L4 checksum handling is a future layer. Leaving CSUM unnegotiated means the device computes full checksums and the driver presents checksummed frames — simpler MVP posture. Revisit when L4 TCP/UDP layer lands.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-001
domain_constraints.description: Frame layout: 6-byte DA, 6-byte SA, 2-byte EtherType/Length, payload, 4-byte FCS.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-002
domain_constraints.description: EtherType ≥ 0x0600 identifies Ethernet II; smaller values are 802.3 Length + LLC header. We support Ethernet II only (LLC/SNAP out of scope).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-003
domain_constraints.description: Minimum frame size: 64 bytes including FCS (512 bit-times). Relaxed to 60 if driver strips FCS.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-004
domain_constraints.description: Maximum frame size: 1518 bytes untagged, 1522 with one VLAN tag. Jumbo-frame rules tracked separately.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-006
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept broadcast DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept multicast DA (group-bit-set) matching configured filter set (virtio-net multicast MAC filter list).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-008
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept unicast DA equal to the device's assigned MAC.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-009
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard frames with incorrect FCS. Typically signaled by virtio host.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-010
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard runt frames (<64 bytes incl. FCS). Guard against runt-length L2 attacks.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-011
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard oversized frames (>1518 untagged, >1522 tagged, >jumbo cap if negotiated).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-012
domain_constraints.description: MUST pad short outgoing frames to the 64-byte minimum. Pad byte value unspecified; convention is zero.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-013
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD zero-fill padding bytes. Conservative choice; avoids info leaks.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-014
domain_constraints.description: Inter-frame gap: 96 bit-times at the negotiated link speed.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.3-2018 §4.2.3.3. N/A — virtio abstracts PHY; host handles IFG.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-015
domain_constraints.description: Source MAC MUST have unicast bit clear (LSb of first byte = 0).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-016
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT emit a frame with source MAC = broadcast or multicast. Sanity check at TX.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-017
domain_constraints.description: MAC address locally-administered bit (2nd LSb of first byte) is informational only; we don't treat L/A MACs differently.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-018
domain_constraints.description: MUST silently discard Ethernet PAUSE frames (EtherType 0x8808, MAC control opcode 0x0001) and increment a rx_pause_dropped counter. Acting on received pause is a separate flow-control module, explicitly deferred per D045's 'stays deferred' list.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-001
domain_constraints.description: 4-byte tag inserted after SA: TPID=0x8100, TCI = 3-bit PCP + 1-bit DEI + 12-bit VID. RX parses unconditionally; TX inserts when tx_request_t.vid != 0.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-002
domain_constraints.description: Tagged-frame EtherType field is at byte offset 16 (not 12). RX re-reads EtherType at offset 16 after tag detection.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-003
domain_constraints.description: VID 0x000 = priority-tagged (no VLAN membership); 0xFFF reserved. VID extracted into per-frame metadata regardless.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-004
domain_constraints.description: PCP field maps to 802.1p priority classes 0–7. Propagated into per-frame metadata; QoS routing is upper-layer.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST silently discard tagged frames on a port that is not VLAN-capable.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.1Q-2022 §8. DEVIATION: D045 reverses this behavior — we parse tagged frames rather than discard. Kept for historical reference; obsoleted by the D045 design.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-006
domain_constraints.description: 802.1ad Q-in-Q (outer TPID 0x88A8). RX recognizes outer + inner tag; extracts both VIDs to metadata. TX can emit Q-in-Q when two vid fields are non-zero (MVP default: one-level tagging).

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-007
domain_constraints.description: 802.1Qbb Priority Flow Control (PFC).
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.1Qbb. DEVIATION: D045 keeps PFC deferred — additive feature, doesn't reshape parser.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-008
domain_constraints.description: VLAN filter management via control queue (VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_VLAN_ADD / _DEL).
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §5.1.6.5.3. DEVIATION: D045 keeps filter management deferred — additive on already-designed control-queue interface. MVP accepts all VIDs.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-001
domain_constraints.description: ARP frame EtherType = 0x0806.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-002
domain_constraints.description: ARP packet: HTYPE=1 (Ethernet), PTYPE=0x0800 (IPv4), HLEN=6, PLEN=4.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-003
domain_constraints.description: OP=1 is REQUEST, OP=2 is REPLY.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-004
domain_constraints.description: MUST answer an ARP REQUEST whose Target Protocol Address equals a local IP.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST update cache entry on REPLY from an address we have a pending REQUEST for.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-006
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD also update cache on any ARP packet whose Sender HW/Protocol Addresses are in the cache (opportunistic).

domain_constraints.name: ARP-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT add a cache entry solely on observed traffic — only on ARP packets.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-008
domain_constraints.description: Cache entries MUST age out after an implementation-defined TTL. Convention: 20 min for complete, 3 min for incomplete.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-009
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD emit gratuitous ARP on interface-up / IP-assignment (ACD per RFC 5227).

domain_constraints.name: ARP-010
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD defend local IP against conflicting claim.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-011
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT respond to REQUEST whose Target Protocol Address is not a local IP.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-003
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_STATUS (bit 16) to observe link state.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-004
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_MQ (bit 22) for multi-queue.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3. DEVIATION per D053: MVP ships single-queue (RX queue 0 + TX queue 1). MQ is a scale-out optimization, not a correctness requirement; deferred until measured single-queue throughput stops meeting the D040 frame-rate targets.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-005
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_CTRL_VQ (bit 17) to expose the control queue. Needed for MAC-filter / MQ / VLAN config.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT negotiate VIRTIO_F_EVENT_IDX (bit 29) unless we implement the event-suppression protocol correctly. Optimization; may skip for MVP.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-001
domain_constraints.description: Split virtqueue has three areas: Descriptor Table, Available Ring, Used Ring. Packed queues out of MVP scope.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-002
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor Table: array of 16-byte descriptors (addr, len, flags, next).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-003
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor flags: NEXT=1, WRITE=2, INDIRECT=4.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-004
domain_constraints.description: Available Ring: idx + ring[] of descriptor-table indices; updated by driver.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-005
domain_constraints.description: Used Ring: idx + ring[] of used-descriptor + length; updated by device.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-006
domain_constraints.description: Queue size MUST be power-of-2, ≤ queue_size (from device), queried from Common config.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-007
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor Table alignment: 16 bytes; Available Ring: 2 bytes; Used Ring: 4 bytes. Modern device uses queue_desc / queue_driver / queue_device registers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-008
domain_constraints.description: Proper memory barriers around ring-index updates.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §2.7.11. Cross-references D039 §2 (DMA/cache coherence); bugs here are of the 'silently corrupts data at high throughput' class, not the 'returns an error' class.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-001
domain_constraints.description: Receive queue index = 0 (single-queue) or 0,2,4,... (MQ).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-002
domain_constraints.description: Pre-populate RX queue with buffers marked WRITE.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-003
domain_constraints.description: Each incoming packet is prefixed with a virtio_net_hdr (length depends on negotiated features).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-004
domain_constraints.description: virtio_net_hdr.flags, .gso_type, .hdr_len, .gso_size, .csum_start, .csum_offset, .num_buffers — our handler MUST at minimum read num_buffers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST handle multi-descriptor RX when num_buffers > 1 (VIRTIO_NET_F_MRG_RXBUF).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-006
domain_constraints.description: After consuming a descriptor, return it to the Available Ring.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-007
domain_constraints.description: Notify the device of returned buffers via Queue Notify register (unless EVENT_IDX suppresses it).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-001
domain_constraints.description: Transmit queue index = 1 (single-queue) or 1,3,5,... (MQ).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-002
domain_constraints.description: Each outgoing packet MUST be preceded by a virtio_net_hdr.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-003
domain_constraints.description: If VIRTIO_NET_F_CSUM not negotiated, virtio_net_hdr.flags.NEEDS_CSUM MUST be 0. Consistent with VIO-F-006 deviation.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-004
domain_constraints.description: If GSO features not negotiated, virtio_net_hdr.gso_type MUST be GSO_NONE. MVP: no GSO.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-005
domain_constraints.description: Submit descriptor chain: (hdr, payload_0, payload_1, …) with only the first writable flag clear (RX flag doesn't apply at TX).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-006
domain_constraints.description: Wait for Used Ring index advancement, then reclaim buffers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-001
domain_constraints.description: Control queue index = MAX_QUEUE_PAIRS * 2 (or 2 for single-queue).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-002
domain_constraints.description: Command format: class + cmd + data + ack; device writes ack byte at end.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-003
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD support VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_MAC_TABLE_SET to populate the multicast filter (paired with VIO-F-005 + VIRTIO_NET_F_CTRL_RX).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-004
domain_constraints.description: If VLAN in scope, SHOULD support VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_VLAN_ADD / _DEL.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-001
domain_constraints.description: MAC address format: 6 bytes, big-endian on the wire.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-002
domain_constraints.description: OUI (first 3 bytes) assigned by IEEE. Locally administered MACs have bit 1 of first byte set.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-003
domain_constraints.description: Multicast MACs: first byte LSb = 1. Broadcast = FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-004
domain_constraints.description: Multicast IPv4 mapping: 01:00:5E:<lower-23-of-IP>. For L3-side, but L2 filter must accept.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-005
domain_constraints.description: Multicast IPv6 mapping: 33:33:<lower-32-of-IPv6>.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-005
domain_constraints.description: FCS = CRC-32 over DA..payload, polynomial 0xedb88320 reflected (IEEE 802.3-2018 §3.2.9). x86_64 uses PCLMULQDQ fold-by-1 when available, slicing-by-8 fallback. AArch64 uses FEAT_CRC32 native. Both arches verified against IEEE 802.3 / zlib vectors. TX-path bswap32+complement conversion for on-the-wire FCS still pending; virtio-net typically offloads FCS.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.3-2018 §3.2.9. D032 crypto-math strategy (ISA-idiomatic, macros-first, constant-time).

performance_constraints.name: fsa-transition-budget
performance_constraints.description: A single FSA transition (handler-body plus dispatch bookkeeping) MUST complete within the budget. The target is tight because real code paths that land on the L2 hot path ride on top of the FSA engine, and blown transition budgets compound across every derived driver.
performance_constraints.measured_via: OSACA static analysis per handler + runtime perf ratchet once FSA engine lands (D040 baseline TBD).

performance_constraints.name: l2-frame-rate-floor
performance_constraints.description: Minimum sustained L2 throughput at which the system must remain correct and non-lossy. Floor is the gating line for the first release; missing this is an SEV-0-class regression.
performance_constraints.measured_via: End-to-end synthetic-traffic run under the pre-push integration suite once L2 TX/RX paths are wired; OSACA per hot instruction window meanwhile.
performance_constraints.rationale: D040 perf-regression ratchet; docs/l2/DESIGN.md §2 Latency/Throughput targets.

performance_constraints.name: l2-frame-rate-target
performance_constraints.description: Target sustained L2 throughput for the shipping product. At 10 Gbps the wire-time budget is ~67 ns for a minimum-size (64-byte) frame and ~1230 ns for a maximum-size (1518-byte) frame (both figures are on-wire, including the 20-byte preamble+SFD+IPG overhead the MAC actually sees). Every cross-cutting feature (CRC, barriers, observability, VLAN handling) must fit inside the relevant per-frame budget for the traffic mix that cell exercises; the perf ratchet (D040) pins both small-frame and large-frame baselines once measured.
performance_constraints.measured_via: Same harness as l2-frame-rate-floor; target is compared against measured sustained rate under a 1518-byte maximum-frame workload.

performance_constraints.name: obs-disabled-path-budget
performance_constraints.description: An OBS_EMIT event site whose verbosity gate is OFF must add no more than this many cycles to the hot path. Enables shipping observability everywhere without eroding frame-rate budgets.
performance_constraints.measured_via: OSACA port-pressure analysis on the OBS_EMIT macro expansion with verbosity gate asserting not-taken; cross-checked with a tight-loop microbenchmark once the macro lands.
performance_constraints.rationale: docs/observability.md pre-D050 proposal; derived from frame-rate-target budget math at 10 Gbps.

performance_constraints.name: obs-category-gated-budget
performance_constraints.description: An OBS_EMIT event site whose verbosity gate is ON but category bitmask is off must add no more than this many cycles. Slightly looser than the disabled-path budget because it performs one additional load + bit-test.
performance_constraints.measured_via: Same harness as obs-disabled-path-budget, with verbosity gate asserting taken and category bitmask asserting not-taken.

modules.name: vm_launcher
modules.responsibility: Launch QEMU or Firecracker VMs in the background, poll for readiness via serial output file, and kill cleanly. QEMU uses -serial file: for direct serial capture; Firecracker has no equivalent so stdout is redirected into serial_path and the VMM's own logger is diverted to a sibling .fc-log file. Handles both x86_64 and aarch64. Maintains a thread-safe Popen registry for safe process lifecycle management. Validates paths and QEMU arguments. Logs all lifecycle events.

classes.name: VMConfig
classes.description: Immutable configuration for launching a VM. Platform is Literal['qemu','firecracker']. Pydantic validators reject path traversal and blocked QEMU arguments.

methods.name: no_traversal
methods.docstring: field_validator: reject '..' in image_path and serial_path, resolve to absolute.

methods.name: no_blocked_args
methods.docstring: field_validator: reject QEMU flags in _BLOCKED_ARGS set.

classes.name: VMHandle
classes.description: Handle to a running VM process. Includes pid, serial_path, stderr_path, arch, platform. Popen object stored separately in _proc_registry.

functions.name: _reject_traversal
functions.docstring: Reject paths with '..' components. Return resolved absolute path.

functions.name: _register_proc
functions.docstring: Register a Popen in the thread-safe registry.

functions.name: _get_proc
functions.docstring: Look up a registered Popen by PID.

functions.name: _unregister_proc
functions.docstring: Remove a Popen from the registry.

functions.name: launch_vm
functions.docstring: Launch a VM in the background. Truncates serial and stderr files. Dispatches to _launch_qemu or _launch_firecracker by platform. Does not block.
postconditions: VM process is running in background

functions.name: _launch_qemu
functions.docstring: Spawn QEMU with -serial file: redirecting the UART straight to serial_path.

functions.name: _launch_firecracker
functions.docstring: Spawn Firecracker via --no-api with a generated JSON config. Redirects stdout to serial_path (Firecracker has no equivalent of -serial file:) and diverts VMM logger lines to a sibling .fc-log so the serial stream stays mostly clean.

functions.name: _firecracker_log_path
functions.docstring: Co-located VMM log path: serial_path + '.fc-log'.

functions.name: _firecracker_config_path
functions.docstring: Co-located JSON config path: serial_path + '.fc-config.json'.

functions.name: _firecracker_vm_id
functions.docstring: Derive a stable per-launch microVM id from Path(serial_path).stem. Tests use unique tmpdirs so the stem is unique per test.

functions.name: _firecracker_config_dict
functions.docstring: Build the JSON config Firecracker expects. drives is empty (no rootfs needed for diagnostic boots), logger is set to a sibling .fc-log file.

functions.name: _firecracker_args
functions.docstring: Build the firecracker --no-api command line: [firecracker, --no-api, --config-file <path>, --id <vm_id>].

functions.name: wait_for_ready
functions.docstring: Poll serial output until marker appears or timeout. Opens file once, reads in binary mode with 4KB chunks, uses sliding window of marker_len bytes to avoid O(N^2) search. Returns True if ready.

functions.name: _kill_via_proc
functions.docstring: Kill using Popen API: terminate, wait 5s, kill if needed. Immune to PID recycling.

functions.name: _try_waitpid
functions.docstring: Try to reap a child process via os.waitpid with WNOHANG. Returns True if reaped or not a child.

functions.name: _signal_pid
functions.docstring: Send signal to PID. Return False if process gone.

functions.name: _poll_pid_exit
functions.docstring: Poll until PID exits or timeout via waitpid + signal check.

functions.name: _kill_via_pid
functions.docstring: Fallback kill using bare PID. SIGTERM, wait, SIGKILL, reap via waitpid.

functions.name: kill_vm
functions.docstring: Kill VM cleanly. Uses Popen API if registered, falls back to bare PID. Unregisters from _proc_registry. Reaps child to prevent zombies.

functions.name: has_kvm
functions.docstring: Check if /dev/kvm is available.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test with mock subprocess. Autouse fixture clears _proc_registry before/after each test. Tests verify: qemu launch registers Popen, stderr captured to file, serial truncated on launch, path traversal rejected, blocked args rejected, platform Literal validated, marker found/timeout in wait_for_ready, kill via Popen vs bare PID fallback, waitpid reaps zombies. Firecracker tests verify --no-api command shape, JSON config file materializes with correct fields (drives, logger, boot-source), config/log/stderr files all created on disk, no-kvm raises RuntimeError. Helper tests cover the path-derivation functions. Random test order verified via pytest-randomly.

modules.name: guest_builder
modules.responsibility: Assemble and link guest images using GNU as cross-toolchains and GNU ld. Selects the correct toolchain based on (arch, platform) -- ELF class depends on boot protocol: x86_64 qemu uses Multiboot1 (ELF32), x86_64 firecracker uses PVH (ELF64). Returns the path to the built binary.

classes.name: Toolchain
classes.description: Assembler path, linker path, and arch-specific as/ld flag lists. Plain Python class (not pydantic) — constructed directly by toolchain_for.

functions.name: build_guest
functions.docstring: Assemble and link the guest image for the given arch/platform. Returns path to the output binary.

functions.name: toolchain_for
functions.docstring: Return the Toolchain (assembler, linker, as_flags, ld_flags) for the (arch, platform) target. Raises ValueError on unsupported combinations.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test toolchain_for with all supported (arch, platform) combos plus rejection cases. Functional test build_guest with a minimal .S file that assembles and links successfully. Test missing-toolchain path by mocking subprocess.run to raise FileNotFoundError.

modules.name: test_runner
modules.responsibility: Orchestrate the full test cycle: build guest, launch VM, wait for ready, run test cases, kill VM, report results. Serial output read in binary mode with errors='replace'. This is the entry point for both pre-commit and CI.

classes.name: TestCase
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-case` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestResult
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-result` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestSuite
classes.description: Collection of test cases for a target

classes.name: SuiteResult
classes.description: Aggregate results for a test suite run. Has all_passed property.

functions.name: run_suite
functions.docstring: Run a complete test suite: build, boot, test, kill, report. Skips gracefully if Firecracker without KVM.

functions.name: check_serial
functions.docstring: Check serial output for expected content. Reads in binary mode with errors='replace'.

functions.name: check_http
functions.docstring: Make HTTP GET request and verify response body matches expected.

functions.name: _build_image
functions.docstring: Build guest image. Return Path on success or TestResult on failure.

functions.name: _boot_and_test
functions.docstring: Wait for ready, run cases, return results.

functions.name: _should_skip
functions.docstring: Return skip reason if suite can't run, or None if runnable.

functions.name: _run_case
functions.docstring: Run a single test case against a booted VM.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test check_serial and check_http with fixtures. Integration test run_suite with mocked build/launch/wait/kill. Test all failure paths: build failure, boot timeout, check mismatch, Firecracker skip without KVM.

modules.name: cli
modules.responsibility: Command-line interface for the test harness. Supports running a single arch/platform combo or the full matrix. Reports pass/fail per test with exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any failure.

functions.name: main
functions.docstring: Entry point. Parse args, run tests, report, return exit code.

functions.name: parse_args
functions.docstring: Parse command-line arguments.

functions.name: _load_suite
functions.docstring: Load a test suite from a JSON file.

functions.name: _print_result
functions.docstring: Print test results for one suite.

modules.test_strategy: Test argument parsing. Test exit codes for all-pass and any-failure scenarios with mocked test_runner. Test suite loading from JSON.

external_dependencies.name: pydantic
external_dependencies.reason: Data models for VMConfig, VMHandle, TestSuite, TestCase, TestResult, SuiteResult. field_validator for path/args validation. Literal type for platform.

verification_cases.name: eth-fcs-primitive
verification_cases.rationale: Unit-level verification of the CRC-32 IEEE 802.3 primitive on both arches against zlib vectors. Virtio-net frame-level FCS behaviour (eth-fcs-virtio) is separate.

verification_cases.name: virtio-init-sequence
verification_cases.rationale: Integration verification of the full Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 device init handshake. Current tracer-bullet marker checks in run_local.sh are the de-facto cover, but a dedicated integration test that asserts on both happy-path markers and each .*_fail endpoint hasn't been written yet.

entities.name: GuestImage
entities.description: An assembled and linked bare-metal binary that boots directly in a VM. Produced by GNU as + GNU ld from arch/<isa>/platform/<vm>/ sources.

properties.name: path
properties.description: Filesystem path to the ELF/flat binary. Validated: no '..' traversal, resolved to absolute.

properties.name: arch
properties.description: Target ISA

properties.name: platform
properties.description: Target VM platform. Literal type -- Pydantic rejects invalid values.

entities.name: VMInstance
entities.description: A running QEMU or Firecracker process hosting a guest image. Launched in the background, polled for readiness, killed cleanly after tests complete. Popen object tracked in a thread-safe registry keyed by PID. Never use timeout to kill QEMU -- background + poll + kill.

properties.name: pid
properties.description: Host OS process ID. Popen object stored separately in _proc_registry (not Pydantic-serializable).

properties.name: serial_path
properties.description: Path to serial output file (-serial file:<path>). Validated: no '..' traversal. Truncated on launch.

properties.name: stderr_path
properties.description: Path to VMM stderr capture file. Created alongside serial_path as <serial_path>.stderr. Truncated on launch.

entities.name: TestCase
entities.description: A single verification to run against a booted VM guest. Checks are performed from the host side -- either parsing serial output or making HTTP requests to the guest.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Human-readable test name

properties.name: check_type
properties.description: serial: check serial output file for expected markers. http: make HTTP request and verify response.

properties.name: expected
properties.description: Expected output or response content

entities.name: TestResult
entities.description: Pass/fail outcome of a single test case.

properties.name: actual
properties.description: Actual output received

properties.name: message
properties.description: Failure detail if not passed

entities.name: FSATransition
entities.description: A single state transition in an FSA automaton under D043's runtime model. One transition is the unit against which FSA_TRANSITION_BUDGET_NS is enforced: handler-body execution plus dispatch bookkeeping.

properties.name: from_state
properties.description: Current state index before the transition.

properties.name: event_code
properties.description: Event that triggered the transition.

properties.name: to_state
properties.description: State index the automaton moves to.

entities.name: EthernetFrame
entities.description: A single Ethernet II frame crossing the L2 boundary. Per ETH-001..ETH-005, fixed 14-byte header + 46..1500 payload + 4-byte FCS. The unit against which the L2 throughput budgets (1 Gbps floor, 10 Gbps target per D040) are enforced on both RX and TX paths.

properties.name: length
properties.description: Total frame length including header and FCS, in bytes. 64..1518 untagged, 64..1522 with VLAN.

properties.name: ether_type
properties.description: Big-endian 16-bit EtherType (≥ 0x0600) or 802.3 length (< 0x0600; out of scope per ETH-002).

entities.name: VirtioNetDevice
entities.description: The virtio-net device exposed by Firecracker at MMIO 0xC0001000 (MEM_32BIT_DEVICES per the VMM layout). The L2 driver owns the full Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 init sequence (reset → ACKNOWLEDGE → DRIVER → feature negotiation → FEATURES_OK → virtqueue init → DRIVER_OK) plus the split-virtqueue descriptor / available / used rings that upper layers consume for RX and TX.

properties.name: mmio_base
properties.description: Physical MMIO base address. 0xC0001000 on Firecracker x86_64 per MEM_32BIT_DEVICES.

properties.name: queue_size
properties.description: Driver-negotiated virtqueue size, min(QueueNumMax, VIRTQ_MAX_SIZE=256). Both RX and TX queues share this value in MVP.

entities.name: ARPPacket
entities.description: A single ARP packet per RFC 826, carried inside an Ethernet frame with EtherType 0x0806. Fixed-layout header (HTYPE, PTYPE, HLEN, PLEN, OP) followed by Sender HW / Sender Protocol / Target HW / Target Protocol addresses.

properties.name: operation
properties.description: OP field: 1=REQUEST, 2=REPLY (RFC 826).

properties.name: sender_hw_addr
properties.description: 6-byte Sender Hardware Address (MAC).

properties.name: sender_protocol_addr
properties.description: 4-byte Sender Protocol Address (IPv4).

properties.name: target_hw_addr
properties.description: 6-byte Target Hardware Address (MAC). Zero-filled on REQUEST, populated on REPLY.

properties.name: target_protocol_addr
properties.description: 4-byte Target Protocol Address (IPv4). Anchor for ARP-004 / ARP-011 local-IP checks.

entities.name: ObservabilityEventSite
entities.description: A single OBS_EMIT macro instance in the runtime code. Each site bears a fixed cost when observability is disabled, a slightly higher cost when enabled but category-gated-off, and the intrinsic emission cost when both verbosity and category pass. Per the observability proposal (docs/observability.md, pre-D050) and D046's assembly-deferral bar, the disabled-path cost must stay inside a tight budget so shipping observability everywhere doesn't erode the L2 frame-rate target.

properties.name: category
properties.description: Category name: core / uart / virtio / fsa / eth / arp / ip / tcp / http / timer / perf_sample / reserved.

properties.name: min_level
properties.description: Lowest verbosity level at which this site emits (0=off, 1=error, 2=warn, 3=info, 4=debug, 5=trace).

relationships.name: boots
relationships.description: A VM instance boots exactly one guest image

relationships.name: runs_against
relationships.description: Test cases run against a booted VM instance

relationships.name: produces
relationships.description: Each test case produces one result

domain_constraints.name: no-native-execution
domain_constraints.description: Assembly code must NEVER be executed natively on the host. All execution happens inside a VM (QEMU or Firecracker).
domain_constraints.rationale: D003 (100% assembly) — host-native execution of bare-metal targets is undefined and dangerous; a VM is the boundary.

domain_constraints.name: clean-vm-kill
domain_constraints.description: Never use timeout to kill QEMU. Launch in background, poll serial output file, then kill cleanly via Popen.terminate/kill when registry entry exists, or bare PID signals with waitpid as fallback. Use -serial file:<path> not -serial mon:stdio.
domain_constraints.rationale: CLAUDE.md QEMU gotchas: `timeout` doesn't flush `-serial file:` output; mon:stdio breaks under stdout redirection.

domain_constraints.name: host-side-verification
domain_constraints.description: All test verification is from the host side. The guest has no test infrastructure inside it -- it just does what it does, and the host observes. Serial output read in binary mode with errors='replace' to handle non-UTF-8.
domain_constraints.rationale: Bare-metal assembly targets can't host pytest; the host observes serial output + exit signals and interprets them as test outcomes.

domain_constraints.name: kvm-required-for-firecracker
domain_constraints.description: Firecracker requires /dev/kvm and only works for the native architecture. Skip gracefully if KVM is not available.
domain_constraints.rationale: GHA hosted arm64 runners don't expose /dev/kvm, so the aarch64/firecracker cell can't run in CI; it's exercised locally via the Pi-side tracer bullet instead.

domain_constraints.name: path-traversal-rejected
domain_constraints.description: All file paths (image_path, serial_path) are validated to reject '..' components and resolved to absolute form. Pydantic field_validator enforces at construction.
domain_constraints.rationale: Defensive against accidental or adversarial path traversal in test-harness inputs; pydantic validation is the single chokepoint all VM configs pass through.

domain_constraints.name: blocked-qemu-args
domain_constraints.description: extra_args on VMConfig are validated against a blocklist of dangerous QEMU flags: -monitor, -vnc, -chardev, -netdev, -nic, -spice. Pydantic field_validator rejects at construction.
domain_constraints.rationale: Blocklist prevents test harnesses from opening monitor/vnc/network surfaces that could expose the host. Noted as brittle (allowlist would be stronger) in Gemini review; developer-tool scope keeps blocklist acceptable for now.

domain_constraints.name: thread-safe-registry
domain_constraints.description: The Popen process registry is protected by threading.Lock. The Python harness runs on Linux where concurrent launch_vm/kill_vm from different threads is a real scenario.
domain_constraints.rationale: Python harness is host-side code running on Linux under stdlib threading; concurrent launch_vm/kill_vm from different threads is a real scenario (pytest-xdist, concurrent integration runs). Bare-metal single-threaded FSA reasoning does NOT apply here per `feedback_tooling_is_not_target.md`.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-001
domain_constraints.description: On init, reset the device by writing 0 to the Device Status MMIO register (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 1). Post-reset state verified via the shared .status_fail endpoint (VIO-003).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-002
domain_constraints.description: Set ACKNOWLEDGE (bit 0) in Status after reset (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 2). Post-ACK state verified via the shared .status_fail endpoint (VIO-003).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-003
domain_constraints.description: Set DRIVER (bit 1) in Status after ACKNOWLEDGE (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 3). Full expected state after steps 1-3 cross-checked via equality compare against ACK|DRIVER (0x03); mismatch routes through .status_fail and VIO-009.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-004
domain_constraints.description: Read device feature bits via DeviceFeaturesSel / DeviceFeatures, select the driver's subset, write it back via DriverFeaturesSel / DriverFeatures (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 4). MVP subset is VIRTIO_F_VERSION_1 only; VIO-F-001 governs it, VIO-F-002 / VIO-F-004 / VIO-F-006 are declared deviations (VIO-F-004 cites D053), and VIO-F-003 / VIO-F-005 / VIO-F-007 are declared spec-only until their consuming L2 code lands.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-005
domain_constraints.description: Set FEATURES_OK (bit 3) in Status after driver-features write (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 5).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-006
domain_constraints.description: Re-read Status; MUST abort init if FEATURES_OK is not still set (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 6). Equality compare against ACK|DRIVER|FEATURES_OK catches both FEATURES_OK-cleared and unexpected stray bits.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-007
domain_constraints.description: Read device-specific config, discover + initialize virtqueues (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 7). Slice 1: QueueSel + QueueNumMax discovery emits QUEUES:RX=<hex> TX=<hex>. Slice 2: QueueNum (clamped to VIRTQ_MAX_SIZE=256), QueueDesc / QueueDriver / QueueDevice physical addresses programmed, QueueReady toggled with read-back verify. Emits QUEUES:READY on success.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 7 + §4.2.2 register semantics. Memory allocation per D043 (static pools).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-008
domain_constraints.description: Set DRIVER_OK (bit 2) in Status; device is now live (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 step 8). Read-back equality-compares against ACK|DRIVER|FEATURES_OK|DRIVER_OK (0x0F); mismatch routes through .driver_ok_fail + VIO-009.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-009
domain_constraints.description: On any fatal driver error, set FAILED (bit 7) in Status (Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2). Shared .set_failed helper is the single halt endpoint for every post-MagicValue failure path — .status_fail, .features_fail_no_v1, .features_fail_rejected, .queue_fail_rx, .queue_fail_tx, .queue_ready_fail, .driver_ok_fail all route through it. .virtio_fail bypasses it: on a MagicValue mismatch the device's register layout is unverified and a Status write would be blind.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-001
domain_constraints.description: MUST negotiate VIRTIO_F_VERSION_1 (bit 32) — we are a modern driver (Virtio 1.2 §6). MVP driver-features subset is VERSION_1 only; everything else (MAC / STATUS / MQ / CSUM / GSO / EVENT_IDX) stays off until the code path that consumes it lands.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-002
domain_constraints.description: MUST negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_MAC (bit 5) if the device offers it, and read MAC from config space (Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3).
domain_constraints.rationale: DEVIATION: MVP feature-negotiation commit (2026-04-19) accepts no device-specific features. MAC read is deferred to the VIO-007 device-config follow-up. Until that lands, the driver acts as if the device did not offer MAC — a locally-administered random MAC would be generated if this shipped. Tracked so it doesn't silently become a latent bug.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-006
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD NOT negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_CSUM / VIRTIO_NET_F_GUEST_CSUM unless our TCP/UDP stack handles partial checksums (Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3).
domain_constraints.rationale: DEVIATION by spec intent: L4 checksum handling is a future layer. Leaving CSUM unnegotiated means the device computes full checksums and the driver presents checksummed frames — simpler MVP posture. Revisit when L4 TCP/UDP layer lands.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-001
domain_constraints.description: Frame layout: 6-byte DA, 6-byte SA, 2-byte EtherType/Length, payload, 4-byte FCS.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-002
domain_constraints.description: EtherType ≥ 0x0600 identifies Ethernet II; smaller values are 802.3 Length + LLC header. We support Ethernet II only (LLC/SNAP out of scope).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-003
domain_constraints.description: Minimum frame size: 64 bytes including FCS (512 bit-times). Relaxed to 60 if driver strips FCS.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-004
domain_constraints.description: Maximum frame size: 1518 bytes untagged, 1522 with one VLAN tag. Jumbo-frame rules tracked separately.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-006
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept broadcast DA FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept multicast DA (group-bit-set) matching configured filter set (virtio-net multicast MAC filter list).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-008
domain_constraints.description: MUST accept unicast DA equal to the device's assigned MAC.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-009
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard frames with incorrect FCS. Typically signaled by virtio host.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-010
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard runt frames (<64 bytes incl. FCS). Guard against runt-length L2 attacks.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-011
domain_constraints.description: MUST discard oversized frames (>1518 untagged, >1522 tagged, >jumbo cap if negotiated).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-012
domain_constraints.description: MUST pad short outgoing frames to the 64-byte minimum. Pad byte value unspecified; convention is zero.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-013
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD zero-fill padding bytes. Conservative choice; avoids info leaks.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-014
domain_constraints.description: Inter-frame gap: 96 bit-times at the negotiated link speed.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.3-2018 §4.2.3.3. N/A — virtio abstracts PHY; host handles IFG.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-015
domain_constraints.description: Source MAC MUST have unicast bit clear (LSb of first byte = 0).

domain_constraints.name: ETH-016
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT emit a frame with source MAC = broadcast or multicast. Sanity check at TX.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-017
domain_constraints.description: MAC address locally-administered bit (2nd LSb of first byte) is informational only; we don't treat L/A MACs differently.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-018
domain_constraints.description: MUST silently discard Ethernet PAUSE frames (EtherType 0x8808, MAC control opcode 0x0001) and increment a rx_pause_dropped counter. Acting on received pause is a separate flow-control module, explicitly deferred per D045's 'stays deferred' list.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-001
domain_constraints.description: 4-byte tag inserted after SA: TPID=0x8100, TCI = 3-bit PCP + 1-bit DEI + 12-bit VID. RX parses unconditionally; TX inserts when tx_request_t.vid != 0.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-002
domain_constraints.description: Tagged-frame EtherType field is at byte offset 16 (not 12). RX re-reads EtherType at offset 16 after tag detection.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-003
domain_constraints.description: VID 0x000 = priority-tagged (no VLAN membership); 0xFFF reserved. VID extracted into per-frame metadata regardless.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-004
domain_constraints.description: PCP field maps to 802.1p priority classes 0–7. Propagated into per-frame metadata; QoS routing is upper-layer.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST silently discard tagged frames on a port that is not VLAN-capable.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.1Q-2022 §8. DEVIATION: D045 reverses this behavior — we parse tagged frames rather than discard. Kept for historical reference; obsoleted by the D045 design.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-006
domain_constraints.description: 802.1ad Q-in-Q (outer TPID 0x88A8). RX recognizes outer + inner tag; extracts both VIDs to metadata. TX can emit Q-in-Q when two vid fields are non-zero (MVP default: one-level tagging).

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-007
domain_constraints.description: 802.1Qbb Priority Flow Control (PFC).
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.1Qbb. DEVIATION: D045 keeps PFC deferred — additive feature, doesn't reshape parser.

domain_constraints.name: VLAN-008
domain_constraints.description: VLAN filter management via control queue (VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_VLAN_ADD / _DEL).
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §5.1.6.5.3. DEVIATION: D045 keeps filter management deferred — additive on already-designed control-queue interface. MVP accepts all VIDs.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-001
domain_constraints.description: ARP frame EtherType = 0x0806.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-002
domain_constraints.description: ARP packet: HTYPE=1 (Ethernet), PTYPE=0x0800 (IPv4), HLEN=6, PLEN=4.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-003
domain_constraints.description: OP=1 is REQUEST, OP=2 is REPLY.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-004
domain_constraints.description: MUST answer an ARP REQUEST whose Target Protocol Address equals a local IP.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST update cache entry on REPLY from an address we have a pending REQUEST for.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-006
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD also update cache on any ARP packet whose Sender HW/Protocol Addresses are in the cache (opportunistic).

domain_constraints.name: ARP-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT add a cache entry solely on observed traffic — only on ARP packets.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-008
domain_constraints.description: Cache entries MUST age out after an implementation-defined TTL. Convention: 20 min for complete, 3 min for incomplete.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-009
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD emit gratuitous ARP on interface-up / IP-assignment (ACD per RFC 5227).

domain_constraints.name: ARP-010
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD defend local IP against conflicting claim.

domain_constraints.name: ARP-011
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT respond to REQUEST whose Target Protocol Address is not a local IP.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-003
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_STATUS (bit 16) to observe link state.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-004
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_MQ (bit 22) for multi-queue.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §5.1.3. DEVIATION per D053: MVP ships single-queue (RX queue 0 + TX queue 1). MQ is a scale-out optimization, not a correctness requirement; deferred until measured single-queue throughput stops meeting the D040 frame-rate targets.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-005
domain_constraints.description: MAY negotiate VIRTIO_NET_F_CTRL_VQ (bit 17) to expose the control queue. Needed for MAC-filter / MQ / VLAN config.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-F-007
domain_constraints.description: MUST NOT negotiate VIRTIO_F_EVENT_IDX (bit 29) unless we implement the event-suppression protocol correctly. Optimization; may skip for MVP.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-001
domain_constraints.description: Split virtqueue has three areas: Descriptor Table, Available Ring, Used Ring. Packed queues out of MVP scope.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-002
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor Table: array of 16-byte descriptors (addr, len, flags, next).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-003
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor flags: NEXT=1, WRITE=2, INDIRECT=4.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-004
domain_constraints.description: Available Ring: idx + ring[] of descriptor-table indices; updated by driver.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-005
domain_constraints.description: Used Ring: idx + ring[] of used-descriptor + length; updated by device.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-006
domain_constraints.description: Queue size MUST be power-of-2, ≤ queue_size (from device), queried from Common config.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-007
domain_constraints.description: Descriptor Table alignment: 16 bytes; Available Ring: 2 bytes; Used Ring: 4 bytes. Modern device uses queue_desc / queue_driver / queue_device registers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-Q-008
domain_constraints.description: Proper memory barriers around ring-index updates.
domain_constraints.rationale: Virtio 1.2 §2.7.11. Cross-references D039 §2 (DMA/cache coherence); bugs here are of the 'silently corrupts data at high throughput' class, not the 'returns an error' class.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-001
domain_constraints.description: Receive queue index = 0 (single-queue) or 0,2,4,... (MQ).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-002
domain_constraints.description: Pre-populate RX queue with buffers marked WRITE.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-003
domain_constraints.description: Each incoming packet is prefixed with a virtio_net_hdr (length depends on negotiated features).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-004
domain_constraints.description: virtio_net_hdr.flags, .gso_type, .hdr_len, .gso_size, .csum_start, .csum_offset, .num_buffers — our handler MUST at minimum read num_buffers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-005
domain_constraints.description: MUST handle multi-descriptor RX when num_buffers > 1 (VIRTIO_NET_F_MRG_RXBUF).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-006
domain_constraints.description: After consuming a descriptor, return it to the Available Ring.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-R-007
domain_constraints.description: Notify the device of returned buffers via Queue Notify register (unless EVENT_IDX suppresses it).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-001
domain_constraints.description: Transmit queue index = 1 (single-queue) or 1,3,5,... (MQ).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-002
domain_constraints.description: Each outgoing packet MUST be preceded by a virtio_net_hdr.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-003
domain_constraints.description: If VIRTIO_NET_F_CSUM not negotiated, virtio_net_hdr.flags.NEEDS_CSUM MUST be 0. Consistent with VIO-F-006 deviation.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-004
domain_constraints.description: If GSO features not negotiated, virtio_net_hdr.gso_type MUST be GSO_NONE. MVP: no GSO.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-005
domain_constraints.description: Submit descriptor chain: (hdr, payload_0, payload_1, …) with only the first writable flag clear (RX flag doesn't apply at TX).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-T-006
domain_constraints.description: Wait for Used Ring index advancement, then reclaim buffers.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-001
domain_constraints.description: Control queue index = MAX_QUEUE_PAIRS * 2 (or 2 for single-queue).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-002
domain_constraints.description: Command format: class + cmd + data + ack; device writes ack byte at end.

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-003
domain_constraints.description: SHOULD support VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_MAC_TABLE_SET to populate the multicast filter (paired with VIO-F-005 + VIRTIO_NET_F_CTRL_RX).

domain_constraints.name: VIO-C-004
domain_constraints.description: If VLAN in scope, SHOULD support VIRTIO_NET_CTRL_VLAN_ADD / _DEL.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-001
domain_constraints.description: MAC address format: 6 bytes, big-endian on the wire.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-002
domain_constraints.description: OUI (first 3 bytes) assigned by IEEE. Locally administered MACs have bit 1 of first byte set.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-003
domain_constraints.description: Multicast MACs: first byte LSb = 1. Broadcast = FF:FF:FF:FF:FF:FF.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-004
domain_constraints.description: Multicast IPv4 mapping: 01:00:5E:<lower-23-of-IP>. For L3-side, but L2 filter must accept.

domain_constraints.name: MAC-005
domain_constraints.description: Multicast IPv6 mapping: 33:33:<lower-32-of-IPv6>.

domain_constraints.name: ETH-005
domain_constraints.description: FCS = CRC-32 over DA..payload, polynomial 0xedb88320 reflected (IEEE 802.3-2018 §3.2.9). x86_64 uses PCLMULQDQ fold-by-1 when available, slicing-by-8 fallback. AArch64 uses FEAT_CRC32 native. Both arches verified against IEEE 802.3 / zlib vectors. TX-path bswap32+complement conversion for on-the-wire FCS still pending; virtio-net typically offloads FCS.
domain_constraints.rationale: IEEE 802.3-2018 §3.2.9. D032 crypto-math strategy (ISA-idiomatic, macros-first, constant-time).

performance_constraints.name: fsa-transition-budget
performance_constraints.description: A single FSA transition (handler-body plus dispatch bookkeeping) MUST complete within the budget. The target is tight because real code paths that land on the L2 hot path ride on top of the FSA engine, and blown transition budgets compound across every derived driver.
performance_constraints.measured_via: OSACA static analysis per handler + runtime perf ratchet once FSA engine lands (D040 baseline TBD).

performance_constraints.name: l2-frame-rate-floor
performance_constraints.description: Minimum sustained L2 throughput at which the system must remain correct and non-lossy. Floor is the gating line for the first release; missing this is an SEV-0-class regression.
performance_constraints.measured_via: End-to-end synthetic-traffic run under the pre-push integration suite once L2 TX/RX paths are wired; OSACA per hot instruction window meanwhile.
performance_constraints.rationale: D040 perf-regression ratchet; docs/l2/DESIGN.md §2 Latency/Throughput targets.

performance_constraints.name: l2-frame-rate-target
performance_constraints.description: Target sustained L2 throughput for the shipping product. At 10 Gbps the wire-time budget is ~67 ns for a minimum-size (64-byte) frame and ~1230 ns for a maximum-size (1518-byte) frame (both figures are on-wire, including the 20-byte preamble+SFD+IPG overhead the MAC actually sees). Every cross-cutting feature (CRC, barriers, observability, VLAN handling) must fit inside the relevant per-frame budget for the traffic mix that cell exercises; the perf ratchet (D040) pins both small-frame and large-frame baselines once measured.
performance_constraints.measured_via: Same harness as l2-frame-rate-floor; target is compared against measured sustained rate under a 1518-byte maximum-frame workload.

performance_constraints.name: obs-disabled-path-budget
performance_constraints.description: An OBS_EMIT event site whose verbosity gate is OFF must add no more than this many cycles to the hot path. Enables shipping observability everywhere without eroding frame-rate budgets.
performance_constraints.measured_via: OSACA port-pressure analysis on the OBS_EMIT macro expansion with verbosity gate asserting not-taken; cross-checked with a tight-loop microbenchmark once the macro lands.
performance_constraints.rationale: docs/observability.md pre-D050 proposal; derived from frame-rate-target budget math at 10 Gbps.

performance_constraints.name: obs-category-gated-budget
performance_constraints.description: An OBS_EMIT event site whose verbosity gate is ON but category bitmask is off must add no more than this many cycles. Slightly looser than the disabled-path budget because it performs one additional load + bit-test.
performance_constraints.measured_via: Same harness as obs-disabled-path-budget, with verbosity gate asserting taken and category bitmask asserting not-taken.

modules.name: vm_launcher
modules.responsibility: Launch QEMU or Firecracker VMs in the background, poll for readiness via serial output file, and kill cleanly. QEMU uses -serial file: for direct serial capture; Firecracker has no equivalent so stdout is redirected into serial_path and the VMM's own logger is diverted to a sibling .fc-log file. Handles both x86_64 and aarch64. Maintains a thread-safe Popen registry for safe process lifecycle management. Validates paths and QEMU arguments. Logs all lifecycle events.

classes.name: VMConfig
classes.description: Immutable configuration for launching a VM. Platform is Literal['qemu','firecracker']. Pydantic validators reject path traversal and blocked QEMU arguments.

methods.name: no_traversal
methods.docstring: field_validator: reject '..' in image_path and serial_path, resolve to absolute.

methods.name: no_blocked_args
methods.docstring: field_validator: reject QEMU flags in _BLOCKED_ARGS set.

classes.name: VMHandle
classes.description: Handle to a running VM process. Includes pid, serial_path, stderr_path, arch, platform. Popen object stored separately in _proc_registry.

functions.name: _reject_traversal
functions.docstring: Reject paths with '..' components. Return resolved absolute path.

functions.name: _register_proc
functions.docstring: Register a Popen in the thread-safe registry.

functions.name: _get_proc
functions.docstring: Look up a registered Popen by PID.

functions.name: _unregister_proc
functions.docstring: Remove a Popen from the registry.

functions.name: launch_vm
functions.docstring: Launch a VM in the background. Truncates serial and stderr files. Dispatches to _launch_qemu or _launch_firecracker by platform. Does not block.
postconditions: VM process is running in background

functions.name: _launch_qemu
functions.docstring: Spawn QEMU with -serial file: redirecting the UART straight to serial_path.

functions.name: _launch_firecracker
functions.docstring: Spawn Firecracker via --no-api with a generated JSON config. Redirects stdout to serial_path (Firecracker has no equivalent of -serial file:) and diverts VMM logger lines to a sibling .fc-log so the serial stream stays mostly clean.

functions.name: _firecracker_log_path
functions.docstring: Co-located VMM log path: serial_path + '.fc-log'.

functions.name: _firecracker_config_path
functions.docstring: Co-located JSON config path: serial_path + '.fc-config.json'.

functions.name: _firecracker_vm_id
functions.docstring: Derive a stable per-launch microVM id from Path(serial_path).stem. Tests use unique tmpdirs so the stem is unique per test.

functions.name: _firecracker_config_dict
functions.docstring: Build the JSON config Firecracker expects. drives is empty (no rootfs needed for diagnostic boots), logger is set to a sibling .fc-log file.

functions.name: _firecracker_args
functions.docstring: Build the firecracker --no-api command line: [firecracker, --no-api, --config-file <path>, --id <vm_id>].

functions.name: wait_for_ready
functions.docstring: Poll serial output until marker appears or timeout. Opens file once, reads in binary mode with 4KB chunks, uses sliding window of marker_len bytes to avoid O(N^2) search. Returns True if ready.

functions.name: _kill_via_proc
functions.docstring: Kill using Popen API: terminate, wait 5s, kill if needed. Immune to PID recycling.

functions.name: _try_waitpid
functions.docstring: Try to reap a child process via os.waitpid with WNOHANG. Returns True if reaped or not a child.

functions.name: _signal_pid
functions.docstring: Send signal to PID. Return False if process gone.

functions.name: _poll_pid_exit
functions.docstring: Poll until PID exits or timeout via waitpid + signal check.

functions.name: _kill_via_pid
functions.docstring: Fallback kill using bare PID. SIGTERM, wait, SIGKILL, reap via waitpid.

functions.name: kill_vm
functions.docstring: Kill VM cleanly. Uses Popen API if registered, falls back to bare PID. Unregisters from _proc_registry. Reaps child to prevent zombies.

functions.name: has_kvm
functions.docstring: Check if /dev/kvm is available.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test with mock subprocess. Autouse fixture clears _proc_registry before/after each test. Tests verify: qemu launch registers Popen, stderr captured to file, serial truncated on launch, path traversal rejected, blocked args rejected, platform Literal validated, marker found/timeout in wait_for_ready, kill via Popen vs bare PID fallback, waitpid reaps zombies. Firecracker tests verify --no-api command shape, JSON config file materializes with correct fields (drives, logger, boot-source), config/log/stderr files all created on disk, no-kvm raises RuntimeError. Helper tests cover the path-derivation functions. Random test order verified via pytest-randomly.

modules.name: guest_builder
modules.responsibility: Assemble and link guest images using GNU as cross-toolchains and GNU ld. Selects the correct toolchain based on (arch, platform) -- ELF class depends on boot protocol: x86_64 qemu uses Multiboot1 (ELF32), x86_64 firecracker uses PVH (ELF64). Returns the path to the built binary.

classes.name: Toolchain
classes.description: Assembler path, linker path, and arch-specific as/ld flag lists. Plain Python class (not pydantic) — constructed directly by toolchain_for.

functions.name: build_guest
functions.docstring: Assemble and link the guest image for the given arch/platform. Returns path to the output binary.

functions.name: toolchain_for
functions.docstring: Return the Toolchain (assembler, linker, as_flags, ld_flags) for the (arch, platform) target. Raises ValueError on unsupported combinations.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test toolchain_for with all supported (arch, platform) combos plus rejection cases. Functional test build_guest with a minimal .S file that assembles and links successfully. Test missing-toolchain path by mocking subprocess.run to raise FileNotFoundError.

modules.name: test_runner
modules.responsibility: Orchestrate the full test cycle: build guest, launch VM, wait for ready, run test cases, kill VM, report results. Serial output read in binary mode with errors='replace'. This is the entry point for both pre-commit and CI.

classes.name: TestCase
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-case` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestResult
classes.description: Pydantic model implementing the `test-result` problem-domain Entity.

classes.name: TestSuite
classes.description: Collection of test cases for a target

classes.name: SuiteResult
classes.description: Aggregate results for a test suite run. Has all_passed property.

functions.name: run_suite
functions.docstring: Run a complete test suite: build, boot, test, kill, report. Skips gracefully if Firecracker without KVM.

functions.name: check_serial
functions.docstring: Check serial output for expected content. Reads in binary mode with errors='replace'.

functions.name: check_http
functions.docstring: Make HTTP GET request and verify response body matches expected.

functions.name: _build_image
functions.docstring: Build guest image. Return Path on success or TestResult on failure.

functions.name: _boot_and_test
functions.docstring: Wait for ready, run cases, return results.

functions.name: _should_skip
functions.docstring: Return skip reason if suite can't run, or None if runnable.

functions.name: _run_case
functions.docstring: Run a single test case against a booted VM.

modules.test_strategy: Unit test check_serial and check_http with fixtures. Integration test run_suite with mocked build/launch/wait/kill. Test all failure paths: build failure, boot timeout, check mismatch, Firecracker skip without KVM.

modules.name: cli
modules.responsibility: Command-line interface for the test harness. Supports running a single arch/platform combo or the full matrix. Reports pass/fail per test with exit code 0 on all-pass, 1 on any failure.

functions.name: main
functions.docstring: Entry point. Parse args, run tests, report, return exit code.

functions.name: parse_args
functions.docstring: Parse command-line arguments.

functions.name: _load_suite
functions.docstring: Load a test suite from a JSON file.

functions.name: _print_result
functions.docstring: Print test results for one suite.

modules.test_strategy: Test argument parsing. Test exit codes for all-pass and any-failure scenarios with mocked test_runner. Test suite loading from JSON.

external_dependencies.name: pydantic
external_dependencies.reason: Data models for VMConfig, VMHandle, TestSuite, TestCase, TestResult, SuiteResult. field_validator for path/args validation. Literal type for platform.

verification_cases.name: eth-fcs-primitive
verification_cases.rationale: Unit-level verification of the CRC-32 IEEE 802.3 primitive on both arches against zlib vectors. Virtio-net frame-level FCS behaviour (eth-fcs-virtio) is separate.

verification_cases.name: virtio-init-sequence
verification_cases.rationale: Integration verification of the full Virtio 1.2 §2.1.2 device init handshake.
